  • Poor reviews about the client

  • Budget too low
  • Not in my preferred location
  • The client will not be notified. Your feedback helps us improve job search.
Hourly: $25-$40 - Expert - Est. Time: Less than 1 month, Less than 30 hrs/week
We are looking for a senior WooCommerce expert to diagnose an intermittent checkout conversion issue. Current situation: - Traffic is strong and stable - Users reach checkout - Checkout starts are high - But purchases intermittently drop to zero during certain time windows - This is NOT a design or optimization task. We need: - someone experienced with WooCommerce checkout issues - experience with payment gateways (Bolt, Stripe, etc.) - ability to diagnose intermittent / non-reproducible issues - experience debugging frontend JS + backend interaction You will NOT be implementing fixes. Your role is: - identify root cause - pinpoint where checkout fails (initiation, progression, payment, or confirmation) - provide clear diagnosis and recommended fix

  • Budget too low
  • Not in my preferred location
  • The client will not be notified. Your feedback helps us improve job search.
Hourly: $14-$35 - Intermediate - Est. Time: Less than 1 month, Less than 30 hrs/week
About the project We're building out a WordPress site using Advanced Custom Fields (ACF) as the backbone for content management. This is a hands-on build where you'll set up field groups, custom post types, and template logic from the ground up. What you'll be doing Setting up and structuring ACF field groups and flexible content layouts Building PHP templates that pull and display ACF data cleanly Connecting custom post types and taxonomies to ACF fields Making sure everything is tidy, readable, and easy for a non-dev to manage in the back end What we're looking for Solid hands-on ACF experience (please share examples) Clean, well-structured PHP/HTML/CSS Good communicator — we work iteratively, so regular check-ins matter Someone who takes ownership and flags issues early Nice to have Experience with ACF Pro (Flexible Content, Repeater fields) Familiarity with custom Gutenberg blocks via ACF This starts as a short-term engagement to see how we work together, with the potential to grow into something longer term. Good vibes and clear communication matter as much as the code.

Web Developer for Real Estate Website Rebuild — IDX Integration, SEO, GEO & Core Web Vitals

  • Just not interested
  • Vague Description
  • Unrealistic Expectations
  • Too Many Applicants
  • Job posted too long ago
  • Poor reviews about the client
  • Doesn't Match Skills
  • I am overqualified
  • Budget too low
  • Not in my preferred location
  • The client will not be notified. Your feedback helps us improve job search.
Hourly: $15-$35 - Intermediate - Est. Time: 3 to 6 months, 30+ hrs/week
About Us: We're a real estate brokerage and property management company with 25+ years in the business. Our current site isn't delivering the search visibility or leads it should, and we're rebuilding it from the ground up. Where We're Starting: We have a strategic framework in place covering site architecture, audience structure, and a phased build plan. It's a starting point, not a finished blueprint. We have no content written yet, and we expect the developer we hire to help shape the direction as much as follow it. If something in our plan doesn't hold up technically or strategically, we want to know. What We Need From You: - CMS setup and secure, scalable backend architecture - IDX/MLS integration property search, listings display, maps, and lead capture - Technical SEO from day one: schema markup, JSON-LD, on-page optimization - GEO and AI search optimization for local and organic visibility Image optimization, compression, semantic file naming, alt text, lazy loading and so on - Page speed and Core Web Vitals performance - Mobile-first responsive design - Integration of a large YouTube video archive spanning 25 years Who We're Looking For: A developer who takes ownership of the technical side and communicates clearly with non-technical clients. You know what good looks like and you'll say so when something could be better. Real estate or service-business experience is a plus. Project Scope Substantial rebuild, roughly 3–6 months with clear milestones. The right fit will be invited into ongoing maintenance and optimization work. To Apply, Please Include: - Examples of relevant work — live URLs and a brief note on your role - Your approach to front end design and technical SEO on a new build - Rate and current availability

WordPress / WooCommerce Checkout Specialist (Stripe + Mobile Debugging)

  • Just not interested
  • Vague Description
  • Unrealistic Expectations
  • Too Many Applicants
  • Job posted too long ago
  • Poor reviews about the client
  • Doesn't Match Skills
  • I am overqualified
  • Budget too low
  • Not in my preferred location
  • The client will not be notified. Your feedback helps us improve job search.
Hourly: $20-$40 - Expert - Est. Time: Less than 1 week, Less than 30 hrs/week
🚨 EXPERT ONLY: Fix WooCommerce Checkout (Stripe + Mobile Bug) We are hiring a senior-level developer ONLY to fix a broken/custom WooCommerce checkout. If you are not experienced with deep debugging (JS + Stripe + WooCommerce), do NOT apply. ❌ Do NOT Apply If: You rely on plugins to solve problems You “try different things” instead of identifying root causes You have never debugged Stripe Elements issues You don’t actively use Chrome DevTools (Network + Console) You cannot clearly explain WHY something is broken Project Overview We have a custom multi-step WooCommerce checkout with the following issues: Stripe fields fail to load on mobile (cellular only) but work on WiFi Checkout has JS/validation inconsistencies Mobile UI bugs (elements stretching, overlapping) Possible caching/CDN or async loading conflicts What We Expect Identify the exact root cause quickly Fix Stripe so it loads consistently on all devices/networks Clean up JavaScript conflicts / execution order issues Fix mobile UI issues properly (not hacks) Deliver a stable, production-ready checkout 💡 REQUIRED SKILLS WooCommerce (custom checkout experience REQUIRED) Stripe (Elements / WooCommerce Stripe plugin) Advanced JavaScript debugging (async, race conditions, network issues) Strong CSS (mobile responsiveness) Experience debugging real production checkout issues 🔥 REQUIRED SCREENING (MANDATORY) If you do NOT answer these, you will be ignored. A checkout works on WiFi but NOT on cellular. → List 3 specific technical causes and how you would test each. Stripe Elements are not rendering. → What are the first 5 things you check? Describe a real checkout bug you fixed (not a basic one). → What was the root cause? What tools do you use to debug: JavaScript issues Network/loading issues Mobile-specific problems 🚫 Auto-Reject Rule Include this exact phrase at the TOP of your proposal: "CHECKOUT FIX EXPERT" If it’s missing, your application will be ignored. 💰 Scope Immediate debugging + fixes More work available if you perform well 🎯 Goal A rock-solid checkout that works flawlessly on mobile (WiFi + cellular) with zero payment failures.
Skills
    StripeWordPressPHPJavaScriptCSSWooCommercePayment Gateway Integration

Crossmint Integration with Wordpress

  • Just not interested
  • Vague Description
  • Unrealistic Expectations
  • Too Many Applicants
  • Job posted too long ago
  • Poor reviews about the client
  • Doesn't Match Skills
  • I am overqualified
  • Budget too low
  • Not in my preferred location
  • The client will not be notified. Your feedback helps us improve job search.
Hourly: $8-$10 - Expert - Est. Time: Less than 1 week, Hours to be determined
We are building an NFT-based artwork platform using Crossmint integrated into a custom WordPress / WooCommerce / Dokan setup. Our minting flow is fully functional in test mode, but we are currently blocked because Crossmint has not approved payments for the live environment. Without this approval, we cannot move forward with production. We are looking for someone with real, hands-on experience with Crossmint, specifically someone who has successfully taken a project from test to live with payments enabled. Your role will be to review our current integration (custom plugin), identify why the approval is being delayed or rejected, and guide us through the exact steps needed to meet Crossmint’s requirements. This may include helping with compliance expectations, configuration fixes, and advising on how to communicate effectively with Crossmint support to get approval faster. This is a short-term troubleshooting and consulting task, but we are open to extending the engagement if needed. When applying, please briefly explain your past experience with Crossmint and whether you have successfully enabled live payments before, along with how you approached it.
Skills
    WordPressCrossmintPHP

WooCommerce Website Speed Optimisation – Mobile PageSpeed / Core Web Vitals / VPS

  • Just not interested
  • Vague Description
  • Unrealistic Expectations
  • Too Many Applicants
  • Job posted too long ago
  • Poor reviews about the client
  • Doesn't Match Skills
  • I am overqualified
  • Budget too low
  • Not in my preferred location
  • The client will not be notified. Your feedback helps us improve job search.
Hourly - Intermediate - Est. Time: Less than 1 month, Less than 30 hrs/week
We are looking for an experienced WordPress / WooCommerce performance specialist to improve the speed and PageSpeed performance of our ecommerce website. Our site is built on: WordPress / WooCommerce Flatsome theme Managed VPS hosting Ecommerce site with product/category pages, basket, checkout, and international traffic We want to improve performance across the whole site, but mobile is the main focus, as this is currently the weaker area and a large share of our traffic is mobile. Current PageSpeed scores are approximately: Mobile: 39 Desktop: 61 We want a proper technical optimisation, not just basic plugin setup. The main objective is to improve: Mobile PageSpeed Insights score Desktop PageSpeed Insights score Core Web Vitals Largest Contentful Paint Interaction to Next Paint Cumulative Layout Shift Overall mobile loading experience Product page and category page performance Basket/cart and checkout usability where possible We are especially interested in improvements that can reduce friction for mobile users and improve ecommerce conversion rate. Current areas likely needing review include: Image optimisation and next-gen formats Lazy loading CSS and JavaScript optimisation Render-blocking resources Font loading Flatsome/theme optimisation WooCommerce scripts and fragments Plugin performance Caching configuration CDN recommendations if appropriate Server/VPS-level performance configuration Mobile-specific layout and loading issues Important: we also use ecommerce functionality where caching needs to be handled carefully, especially basket/cart/checkout and any dynamic features such as currency or tax display. We do not want changes that break WooCommerce functionality. Required experience: Strong WordPress and WooCommerce speed optimisation experience Proven experience improving PageSpeed / Core Web Vitals Experience with mobile-specific performance improvements Experience with Flatsome or similar WooCommerce themes Ability to diagnose plugin/theme/server bottlenecks Experience with VPS hosting environments Understanding of WooCommerce caching exclusions and dynamic ecommerce pages Ability to test before and after changes clearly Deliverables: Initial audit of current website performance Clear list of issues found Implementation of agreed speed improvements Before/after PageSpeed or Lighthouse results for both mobile and desktop Confirmation that product pages, cart, checkout, and key ecommerce functions still work correctly Notes on any ongoing recommendations or limitations Please include in your proposal: Examples of WooCommerce sites you have optimised Before/after PageSpeed or Core Web Vitals results, if available Your approach to mobile-focused performance improvements How you handle WooCommerce caching safely Whether you work at theme/plugin level, server level, or both Estimated timeline and fixed price or hourly estimate We are looking for someone who can make meaningful, safe improvements to site speed and ecommerce performance, with mobile as the main priority, without breaking key WooCommerce functionality.
Skills
    WordPressPHPWeb DevelopmentPage Speed OptimizationCSSWooCommerceJavaScript

  • The client will not be notified. Your feedback helps us improve job search.
Fixed-price - Intermediate - Est. Budget: $300
We're a commercial real estate firm looking for an experienced WordPress developer to add an interactive map feature to one page of our existing website. The project is straightforward but we want it done cleanly and professionally. **What We Need** - Recommend a map solution (plugin or third-party tool) that: - Allows us to add, edit, and remove property locations ourselves — no developer needed for ongoing updates - Supports basic style customization (colors, fonts, or map theme) to match our branding - Is reliable and well-supported (not abandoned plugins) - Integrate your recommended solution into our WordPress site - Add and style the map on one designated page - Set up the location management interface so our non-technical team can use it easily - Brief us on how to manage locations going forward **Project Scope** This is a small, fixed-scope project. We expect it to be completable in a few hours for someone with the right experience. Please include in your proposal: 1. Which map solution you'd recommend and why 2. Examples of similar work you've done 3. Your fixed-price quote and estimated turnaround

  • The client will not be notified. Your feedback helps us improve job search.
Hourly: $30-$40 - Expert - Est. Time: 3 to 6 months, 30+ hrs/week
We are a WordPress-focused development team working primarily with agencies and complex client projects. We’re looking to expand our bench with a high-level developer who is comfortable working on custom, non-trivial WordPress / WooCommerce builds - not template-based or plugin-stacking work. This is a test post to identify developers for ongoing collaboration. What we typically work on Our projects are not standard brochure sites. Most involve: Custom WooCommerce functionality (checkout flows, payments, subscriptions, logic) Performance optimization (Core Web Vitals, real traffic, not just Lighthouse scores) Large or complex websites (multi-language, multi-site, high content volume) Advanced ACF usage (Options Pages, flexible content, structured systems) Gutenberg / custom blocks (not just classic editor or builders) API integrations (CRMs, marketing tools, automation systems) Debugging complex issues (plugin conflicts, server-level issues, database problems) What this is NOT Not Elementor-only work Not “install a theme and tweak it” Not short-term one-off fixes We’re specifically looking for developers who: Understand when to use WordPress - and when to go beyond default behavior Can build custom functionality using hooks, filters, and proper architecture Think in systems, not pages Requirements 4+ years of WordPress / WooCommerce experience Strong PHP, JavaScript, MySQL Experience with custom themes / plugins Solid understanding of performance and scalability Good communication (written English is enough) How we work Long-term collaboration (many of our projects run for months/years) Direct communication (Slack / task managers) Clear structure and expectations Focus on quality over quantity

  • Vague Description

  • Job posted too long ago
  • Poor reviews about the client
  • Doesn't Match Skills
  • I am overqualified
  • Budget too low
  • Not in my preferred location
  • The client will not be notified. Your feedback helps us improve job search.
Hourly: $20-$40 - Expert - Est. Time: Less than 1 week, Less than 30 hrs/week
Project: Gravity Forms → Meta Pixel Conversion Tracking Site: somoloco.com (WordPress) Goal: When a visitor submits a specific Gravity Form, a Meta Pixel event fires in-browser (e.g. Lead or CompleteRegistration). This event must appear in Meta Events Manager and be usable as a conversion event in Meta Ads campaigns. Current setup: WordPress with Gravity Forms (Stripe integration active) Conversion Bridge plugin installed Meta Pixel ID already in hand Conversion Bridge has a Platforms tab and Integrations tab — unclear if GF integration is available at current license tier What I need you to do: Confirm whether Conversion Bridge's current license supports Gravity Forms integration — if not, recommend the simplest alternative (e.g. GF confirmation page redirect + Page Load trigger, or lightweight custom JS) Configure the Meta Pixel to fire the correct event on GF form submission — specifically on the checkout/inquiry form Verify it's working using Meta's Test Events tool (screenshot confirmation required) No duplicate pixel firing — check for any existing pixel code on site before adding anything new Out of scope: Conversions API / server-side tracking (not needed now) Google Ads tracking (separate project) Any changes to the Gravity Forms form itself Deliverable: Working Meta Pixel event firing on form submission, confirmed in Meta Events Manager Test Events tool Brief note on what was done so my developer on retainer (Muhammad) can maintain it Skills needed: WordPress, Gravity Forms, Meta Pixel, basic JavaScript Estimated time: 1–3 hours

WooCommerce Auto Currency Detection Setup

  • Just not interested
  • Vague Description
  • Unrealistic Expectations
  • Too Many Applicants
  • Job posted too long ago
  • Poor reviews about the client
  • Doesn't Match Skills
  • I am overqualified
  • Budget too low
  • Not in my preferred location
  • The client will not be notified. Your feedback helps us improve job search.
Hourly - Intermediate - Est. Time: Less than 1 month, Less than 30 hrs/week
We are looking for an experienced WooCommerce developer to fully configure reliable automatic currency detection on our ecommerce website. Our site is built on: WordPress / WooCommerce Flatsome theme Managed VPS hosting CURCY multi-currency plugin, Pro licence already active We sell internationally, but the business is only VAT registered in the UK. This adds an important tax/pricing requirement: UK customers are charged UK VAT at 20% International customers are not charged UK VAT International customers effectively see/pay prices without UK VAT, as they are responsible for any local VAT/import duties in their destination country Currency detection and display need to work cleanly alongside this VAT setup We have already tried using the CURCY plugin, but it has not been reliable enough. We have seen issues such as: Page caching causing currency conflicts Basket/cart sometimes showing the wrong currency Product pages switching back to GBP unexpectedly Currency not remaining consistent across the browsing journey General unreliability with geo-detection and cached pages We need someone who can properly diagnose and implement a robust solution, not just toggle a few plugin settings. The ideal outcome is: Automatic currency detection based on customer location Correct currency shown on product/category pages where appropriate Consistent currency behaviour across product pages, cart, mini-cart, and basket UK VAT applied correctly for UK customers UK VAT removed for international customers No caching conflicts causing incorrect currency display Clear testing across multiple countries/locations Checkout can switch back to GBP if needed, as taking final payment in GBP may be preferable A stable setup that works reliably on a live WooCommerce store We are open to using CURCY if it can be configured properly, but we are also open to alternative plugin or technical recommendations if there is a more robust approach. Please include in your proposal: Examples of WooCommerce multi-currency setups you have completed How you would approach caching conflicts with currency detection Whether you recommend using CURCY or an alternative solution How you would handle the UK VAT vs international no-UK-VAT pricing requirement Estimated timeline and fixed price or hourly estimate We are looking for someone who can complete this properly and make the setup reliable, rather than applying a quick temporary fix.
Skills
    Web DesignBug FixDesign EnhancementStripePayPalWooCommerceWordPressWeb DevelopmentPHP

WPML Multilingual Translation – WooCommerce + Kadence Theme (IT → EN, FR, DE)

  • Just not interested
  • Vague Description
  • Unrealistic Expectations
  • Too Many Applicants
  • Job posted too long ago
  • Poor reviews about the client
  • Doesn't Match Skills
  • I am overqualified
  • Budget too low
  • Not in my preferred location
  • The client will not be notified. Your feedback helps us improve job search.
Hourly - Intermediate - Est. Time: Less than 1 month, Less than 30 hrs/week
**Project Overview** We need an experienced WordPress/WooCommerce developer to translate an existing Italian e-commerce website into 3 languages (English, French, German) using WPML. The website sells fresh citrus fruits and has many custom elements, dynamic components, and non-standard layouts that require manual handling beyond bulk translation. **Tech Stack** - WordPress + WooCommerce - Theme: Kadence + Kadence Blocks - Plugin: WPML (already installed and configured) - SEO Plugin: AIOSEO - Other plugins: WooCommerce Subscriptions, Stripe, PayPal **Site Content** - ~20 products - ~40+ blog articles - Several static pages (Home, About, Contacts, Cookie Policy, T&C) **Requirements** - Proven experience with WPML on WooCommerce sites - Strong knowledge of multilingual SEO (hreflang, URL slugs, meta tags) - Experience with Kadence theme and Kadence Blocks - Experience with AIOSEO multilingual setup - Familiarity with schema markup (Product, Article, BreadcrumbList) - Ability to handle custom and dynamic elements not automatically detected by WPML - Attention to detail and ability to meet deadlines **Scope of Work** Milestone 1 — English (EN) - Full product translation: title, short/full description, FAQs - URL slugs (e.g. /en/product-name/) - Meta title and meta description for all products - Image alt texts - Product categories - Blog articles (title, content, meta, slug) - Static pages - WooCommerce strings (checkout, buttons, messages) - Transactional emails (order confirmation, shipping, etc.) - Navigation menu items Milestone 2 — French (FR) Same scope as Milestone 1, fully in French. Milestone 3 — German (DE) Same scope as Milestone 1, fully in German. Milestone 4 — QA, SEO & Final Delivery - Hreflang implementation and validation: x-default → test.it/en/ it-IT → test.it/ de-DE → test.it/de-de/ fr-FR → test.it/fr-fr/ - URL slugs verification for all languages - Schema markup check (Product, Article, BreadcrumbList) - Multilingual sitemap verification - Transactional email test for each language - Checkout flow test for each language - Language switcher test on frontend - Currency switch test (EUR/GBP) - Meta title and description review across all translated pages - Final report with summary of completed work and any issues found **Important Notes** - Each milestone must be reviewed and approved before payment is released - Weekly check-in required **Please include in your proposal:** - Examples of WPML multilingual projects you have worked on - Your experience with Kadence theme - Your approach to handling custom/dynamic elements not detected by WPML - Confirmation that you can start immediately

WordPress Maintenance & Technical SEO Execution Specialist (Elementor Expert)

  • Just not interested
  • Vague Description
  • Unrealistic Expectations
  • Too Many Applicants
  • Job posted too long ago
  • Poor reviews about the client
  • Doesn't Match Skills
  • I am overqualified
  • Budget too low
  • Not in my preferred location
  • The client will not be notified. Your feedback helps us improve job search.
Hourly: $8-$25 - Expert - Est. Time: 3 to 6 months, Less than 30 hrs/week
Job Description: We are managing a portfolio of 9 WordPress websites, all built with Elementor Pro, hosted on WP Engine. We have automated core monitoring and backups via ManageWP. We are looking for an Elementor expert to handle routine maintenance and technical SEO execution. Key Responsibilities: Routine Maintenance (5 hrs/mo - Fixed): Weekly oversight of plugin/theme updates and backup verification. You must perform updates in our staging environment (WP Engine) before production deployment to ensure site stability. Technical SEO Execution (Up to 6 hrs/mo - On-Demand): Implement technical fixes requested by our SEO Manager. This includes bulk metadata updates, schema markup, structural adjustments, and fixing Elementor-specific layout issues. Project-Based Development (Variable): Larger projects (e.g., new site launches using templates, major theme overhauls) will be estimated separately as distinct projects. Requirements & Proficiency (Please rate yourself 1–5 stars in your cover letter): Elementor Pro Mastery: Theme Builder, dynamic tags, global templates, and troubleshooting Elementor-specific CSS/layout conflicts. Technical SEO Execution: Experience implementing SEO-driven fixes (metadata, schema, etc.). WP Engine Environment: Proven experience with staging/production workflows. Communication: Ability to provide estimates before starting any "On-Demand" or "Project" work. Workload & Budget: Retainer Model: We operate on an 11-hour monthly retainer (5h Maintenance + Up to 6h SEO Execution). Note: The 6h SEO execution is an "On-Demand pool." Work volume fluctuates. We request tasks as they arise; you provide an estimate, and we approve before you start. Projects: Any work exceeding the retainer capacity (e.g., site launches) will be treated as separate, project-based tasks. To Apply: Please share: Your experience with Elementor Pro maintenance. A brief example of a technical SEO fix you have implemented. Your hourly rate for retainer work vs. project-based development.
Skills
    WordPressHTMLPHPCSSElementor

Hourly - Expert - Est. Time: Less than 1 week, not_sure
DO NOT CONTACT ME OUTSIDE OF UPWORK OR YOU WILL LOSE THE WORK. I need someone to look into a wordpress issue I have with a website where the elementor page editiors are very slow and the dev server keeps crashing. Need to diagnose and fix the issue to help with sit speed and allow team to make changes to website as it takes so long to load pages to edit that we can't change anything currently. Let me know rates. Cheers

WordPress + WooCommerce developer for booking-based marketplace (Figma ready)

  • Just not interested
  • Vague Description
  • Unrealistic Expectations
  • Too Many Applicants
  • Job posted too long ago
  • Poor reviews about the client
  • Doesn't Match Skills
  • I am overqualified
  • Budget too low
  • Not in my preferred location
  • The client will not be notified. Your feedback helps us improve job search.
Hourly: $12-$35 - Intermediate - Est. Time: Less than 1 month, Less than 30 hrs/week
Join Us: WordPress Developer for Wellness Platform Be part of an exciting rebuild of a wellness services booking platform in the thriving healing and spiritual space, powered by WordPress. You'll start by building the New York site from a polished Figma design, then extend the same architecture and templates to future locations as we grow. From day one, you'll implement a robust WordPress multisite foundation. Create one powerful system with multiple domains, shared templates, unified architecture, and a seamless admin experience. The first site sets the foundation, but your work will enable smooth rollouts for future locations—no repetitive rebuilding. Our current system is tough to manage and extend. We're looking for a developer who can engineer a clean, scalable foundation—not just assemble pages. --- What You'll Be Doing Build the first location site from Figma Set up WordPress multisite architecture Configure WooCommerce as the booking and service backbone Create reusable page templates that can be used across locations Create a reusable service / product page template Create a service directory / catalogue page Support direct booking and payment flow Support request-based booking flow for services that are not directly bookable Set up standard WooCommerce transactional emails Build clean admin workflows for adding services, categories, and pages Set up bulk upload / import for the service catalogue (CSV or Excel) Set up spam-protected forms Install and verify GA4, Google Search Console, and Meta Pixel Set up SEO basics including metadata, schema, XML sitemap, robots.txt, and redirects layer Build mobile-first, accessible, performance-conscious pages --- Pages You’ll Build Static pages Homepage Menu / service directory Category template applied to multiple categories (for example Groups, Couples, Online, Corporate) Concierge form page About Cancellation policy FAQ Gift card landing page Contact Privacy policy Dynamic templates Individual service / product page Service directory / catalogue Blog index Individual blog post Checkout Sign in / register / account dashboard if included --- Bonus Skills (Let Us Know!) Please mention which of these you can handle and how you would approach them Email platform integration with Klaviyo, ActiveCampaign, or similar Custom email capture log inside WordPress with timestamp, source, and consent flag Quote approval flow where users submit a request, admin approves, then user receives a payment link Customer dashboard with booking history, gift card balance, and account details Gift cards through WooCommerce Deposits or partial payments Optimized checkout with abandoned cart automation Affiliate or shop-style page for recommended products WhatsApp click-to-chat with page-specific prefilled messages High-risk payment gateway support such as Authorize.net or NMI if Stripe is not viable CRM integration with Bitrix, Manegea, ActiveCampaign, or similar --- Where This Can Lead After the first site is live, additional locations will be added into the same multisite structure. One of these migrations will require careful SEO handling, including URL inventory, 301 redirect mapping, staging validation, and post-launch monitoring. The goal is to avoid rebuilding and keep all locations aligned on the same templates and system. Apply only if you truly understand why multisite architecture matters for this kind of project—and are excited to own that challenge.
Skills
    Payment Gateway IntegrationWordPressWooCommercePHPCSSWeb DevelopmentJavaScriptHTMLSEO Setup & Configuration

Hourly - Intermediate - Est. Time: Less than 1 week, Less than 30 hrs/week
We need a specialist who can automate the process of taking listings from platforms like LoopNet and CoStar and automate publishing them to a WordPress website. This is not a manual posting role. We are looking for someone who can think in systems, reduce repetitive work, and build a reliable workflow. Scope of Work: Extract listing data from LoopNet and/or CoStar Structure and normalize property data (price, location, specs, images, etc.) Automatically publish listings to a WordPress site Map data into custom fields (ACF or similar) Ensure listings display cleanly and consistently on the front end Set up a process for ongoing syncing or easy updates Troubleshoot formatting, images, and data inconsistencies

Rebuild Elementor WordPress Website Into a Faster Custom-Coded Site

  • Just not interested
  • Vague Description
  • Unrealistic Expectations
  • Too Many Applicants
  • Job posted too long ago
  • Poor reviews about the client
  • Doesn't Match Skills
  • I am overqualified
  • Budget too low
  • Not in my preferred location
  • The client will not be notified. Your feedback helps us improve job search.
Fixed-price - Intermediate - Est. Budget: $1,500
We have an existing WordPress website built with Elementor. The design and content are already in place, but the site is slower than we want and relies too heavily on page-builder code. We are looking for an experienced WordPress developer to rebuild the site using a faster, cleaner, custom-coded approach. We are happy to keep the site on WordPress if that is the best option, but we want to remove as much Elementor bloat as possible. The ideal setup is likely a lightweight custom WordPress theme using clean PHP, HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and either Gutenberg blocks or ACF so our team can still edit content easily. Main Goals: Rebuild the current design without Elementor bloat Improve site speed and Core Web Vitals Keep the website editable in WordPress Preserve SEO, URLs, metadata, headings, schema, and tracking Make the site fully responsive on desktop, tablet, and mobile Use minimal plugins and clean, maintainable code Launch safely from staging to live Deliverables: Custom lightweight WordPress rebuild Editable content sections Mobile-responsive pages Performance optimization SEO-safe migration Staging setup and live deployment Basic handover notes Please Apply If You Have Experience With: Custom WordPress theme development Rebuilding Elementor sites WordPress performance optimization Core Web Vitals Gutenberg and/or ACF Technical SEO Safe website migrations In Your Proposal, Please Include: Examples of Elementor sites you have rebuilt or optimized Your recommended approach for this project Whether you would keep the site on WordPress and why How you would keep the site editable for our team Estimated timeline and cost

Title: WooCommerce Telehealth Developer — OpenLoop API Integration (Subscription + Funnel)

  • Just not interested
  • Vague Description
  • Unrealistic Expectations
  • Too Many Applicants
  • Job posted too long ago
  • Poor reviews about the client
  • Doesn't Match Skills
  • I am overqualified
  • Budget too low
  • Not in my preferred location
  • The client will not be notified. Your feedback helps us improve job search.
Hourly - Expert - Est. Time: 3 to 6 months, Less than 30 hrs/week
Title: WooCommerce Telehealth Developer — OpenLoop API Integration (Subscription + Funnel) Budget: $3,000–$5,000 Fixed Price Timeline: 6–8 Weeks --- ## Overview We are building a direct-to-consumer telehealth platform and need a strong WooCommerce developer to execute a clean, high-performing build with API integration. This is a cash-pay subscription model (no insurance) with a focus on: - Weight loss (GLP-1) - Hormone optimization - Peptide therapies We are NOT looking for a basic WordPress designer. We need someone comfortable with subscriptions, APIs, and real system integration. --- ## What You’ll Build - WordPress + WooCommerce site (custom theme or Elementor — your recommendation) - Subscription billing (WooCommerce Subscriptions + Stripe) - Product catalog (GLP-1, peptides, men’s + women’s health) - Multi-step intake / assessment (pre-checkout) - OpenLoop API integration: - Send patient + order data post-checkout - Receive updates via webhooks (order status, fulfillment, etc.) - Patient portal (OpenLoop native OR lightweight WP-based solution) - Mobile-first responsive design - Clean, minimal UX (not overdesigned) - Basic SEO setup --- ## Reference Sites (Study Before Applying) - joinfridays.com - home.medvi.org We are looking for similar flow, simplicity, and clarity. --- ## Required Experience - WooCommerce Subscriptions (must have built recurring billing systems) - Strong REST API experience (sending + receiving data, webhooks) - Comfortable reading and implementing external API documentation - Experience building structured checkout flows - Ability to build clean, fast, mobile-first sites --- ## Nice to Have - Experience with OpenLoop Health - Healthcare / telehealth builds - HIPAA-aware environments (BAA familiarity) - Quiz funnels (Typeform, WPForms, or custom) --- ## Deliverables 1. Fully functional WooCommerce site (production-ready) 2. Subscription billing live and tested 3. Intake form integrated into checkout flow 4. OpenLoop API fully connected (sandbox tested) 5. Webhook handling for order updates 6. Staging environment before launch 7. 30 days post-launch support --- ## Important — Application Requirement To apply, include: 1. Links to 2–3 WooCommerce sites you’ve built (subscriptions preferred) 2. Brief explanation (2–3 sentences): How would you pass WooCommerce order data to an external API and handle webhook responses? 3. Your recommended stack (Elementor vs custom theme, form solution, etc.) 4. Availability over the next 6–8 weeks --- We are looking for someone who can execute quickly and cleanly. Strong performance here can lead to ongoing work.

  • Just not interested
  • Vague Description
  • Unrealistic Expectations
  • Too Many Applicants
  • Job posted too long ago
  • Poor reviews about the client
  • Doesn't Match Skills
  • I am overqualified
  • Budget too low
  • Not in my preferred location
  • The client will not be notified. Your feedback helps us improve job search.
Hourly: $15-$20 - Intermediate - Est. Time: More than 6 months, Less than 30 hrs/week
Scope of Work 1. Routine Maintenance (Weekly/Monthly) WordPress core updates – Apply minor/patch updates promptly; test major releases in staging before deploying. Plugin & theme updates – Monitor for compatibility, apply security patches, and resolve any conflicts. Daily automated backups – Ensure backups are running and stored off‑server (e.g., cloud or external storage). Security monitoring – Maintain Wordfence or equivalent firewall; review logs; block suspicious IPs; run weekly malware scans. Performance optimization – Monitor page load speeds, database optimization, image compression, and caching configuration. 2. Content & Support (As‑Needed) Quick content updates – Add or edit text/images on existing pages (e.g., event details, sponsor logos). Troubleshooting – Address login issues, form errors, or MemberPress transaction problems within 24‑48 hours. Staging environment – Use a staging copy for any non‑trivial changes before pushing live. Required Qualifications Proven experience with WordPress + MemberPress sites, especially membership and e‑commerce workflows. Familiarity with Hostinger hosting environment (hPanel, file manager, staging). Understanding of nonprofit/501(c)(3) website needs (donation flows, member portals, event calendars). Ability to communicate clearly and provide monthly activity reports.

Hourly - Intermediate - Est. Time: Less than 1 week, Less than 30 hrs/week
We've recently been experiencing loading issues with our WP site. We have JetPack set up on the site and are getting several notices a day that the site is unreachable. It seems to be an intermittent issue, I have occasionally experienced slow loads or no load with a 503 error. We recently switched to and are hosting with Bluehost on a shared server. They assure me it is not a server issue. The timing of the issue does not line up exactly with the switch to BlueHost. I'm not sure how best to describe the issue to give you the best view of what's going on. If you think you'd like to help us out and would like more info, let me know what you need.

  • Poor reviews about the client

  • I am overqualified
  • Budget too low
  • Not in my preferred location
  • The client will not be notified. Your feedback helps us improve job search.
Hourly: $10-$40 - Expert - Est. Time: 3 to 6 months, 30+ hrs/week
I have several websites that are WORDPRESS built. They mostly use Elementor, but sometimes do not. I need to transition them from Wordpress into normal, clean, HTML + CSS. That is goal number one. Goal number two is putting it into 11ty + nunjucks, but that is just an added bonus. I have been playing around and vibe coding, but I cannot seem to get a very good pixel perfect match when I am trying to refactor the code. It might not need to be "pixel perfect" but it needs to be almost the same as the original site that is built in WordPress. I'm looking for someone that is capable in doing this. Ideally, they help us build the automated engine as we have many clients that are looking to "switch" off of Wordpress. In the screening question, please give me those two sample links that I am asking for. It needs to be of the SAME website, so that I can see a side by side and judge the quality of the code in the "clean" version.

  • Too Many Applicants
  • Job posted too long ago
  • Poor reviews about the client
  • Doesn't Match Skills
  • I am overqualified
  • Budget too low
  • Not in my preferred location
  • The client will not be notified. Your feedback helps us improve job search.
Hourly: $12-$35 - Intermediate - Est. Time: More than 6 months, 30+ hrs/week
E-commerce Developer, Automator, and Integrator (WordPress, APIs, Automation, AI-Driven Workflows) *** This role offers a unique opportunity to gain broad, hands-on experience with cutting-edge automation, AI, API integrations, and e-commerce systems. *** Overview We are a portfolio of Electrical Distribution companies operating in a fast-paced, technology-driven environment, where speed, adaptability, and innovation are critical. Our IT team is highly motivated and forward-thinking, focused on building scalable, modern solutions across multiple platforms and brands. We are seeking a highly capable E-commerce Developer who can operate independently, think critically, and contribute to cutting-edge initiatives in automation, AI, and e-commerce. This role requires someone who can keep pace with rapid development cycles and proactively drive solutions forward. This is not a task-based role. You will not be waiting for instructions, you will be expected to analyze problems, recommend solutions, and execute with confidence. Our environment combines: API JSON SQL integrations Automation (n8n) and others AI-assisted development (Claude, OpenAI, etc) WordPress / WooCommerce If you are someone who thrives in fast-moving environments and enjoys leveraging AI + automation to scale output, this role is for you. What You’ll Be Doing Build and customize WordPress / WooCommerce functionality Develop and maintain API integrations (REST, JSON-based) Design and implement automation workflows using n8n Leverage AI tools (Claude, OpenAI) to accelerate development and problem-solving Write and optimize MySQL queries for performance and data workflows Troubleshoot and improve Linux server performance Create custom frontend and backend solutions using appropriate coding languages Contribute to architecture decisions and propose improvements Participate in team meetings, provide updates, and actively suggest better approaches Required Skills Strong experience with: WordPress (custom development, not just plugins) PHP, JavaScript, HTML, CSS MySQL (query writing, optimization, connecting) REST APIs + JSON Experience or strong interest in: Automation tools like n8n AI tools like Claude, OpenAI Linux server familiarity (performance, debugging, deployments) Ability to learn quickly and apply new technologies immediately Strong problem-solving and critical thinking skills High attention to detail Nice to Have Good understanding of standard design Experience scaling e-commerce platforms Familiarity with performance optimization (frontend + backend) Experience working with distributed/remote teams Work Style Expectations Able to work independently with minimal direction Comfortable making decisions and owning outcomes Strong communicator in spoken and written English Must attend morning (U.S.-based) meetings Actively contributes ideas and not just executes tasks Who This Is NOT For Developers who need step-by-step instructions Candidates who rely entirely on being trained Low-initiative or passive contributors Who This IS For Self-driven builders who take responsibility from idea to execution Developers who use AI to multiply their output People who want to grow with evolving technology Individuals who enjoy solving real business problems Technologists eager to learn and adopt emerging technologies Upcoming Projects Build real-time ERP bidirectional website integrations across multiple brands Deploy AI-powered natural language search for advanced product discovery Fully automate inventory, pricing, and product pipelines to e-commerce platforms Develop AI agents to generate insights, automate decisions, and drive operations Create a customer portal for order tracking and account visibility Develop API-driven order history services for internal and customer-facing applications Growth & Work Style Expectations We are willing to educate and provide guidance, but the right candidate must be coachable, proactive, and eager to learn through doing. We are not looking for someone who waits for step-by-step instruction or asks to be taught everything upfront. Instead, we value individuals who: Dive in, experiment, and learn by solving real problems Use available tools (including AI) to figure things out independently Ask thoughtful questions when stuck, not before trying Take ownership of their work and push tasks forward without constant direction You don’t need to know everything but you do need the ability to think critically, problem-solve, and work independently to deliver results. There will absolutely be team support and collaboration, but success in this role comes from your ability to take initiative and execute.

Fixed-price - Intermediate - Est. Budget: $200
I’m looking for a WordPress freelancer to build an interactive image hotspot feature. I have one main image that contains multiple book covers. I would like each book cover to be clickable. When a user hovers over or clicks a specific book cover, a small popup/modal should appear with the book title, short description, and a button/link to the page where the book can be purchased. The solution should work well on desktop and mobile, be easy for me to update in WordPress, and ideally use a lightweight plugin or custom HTML/CSS/JavaScript. Required skills: WordPress, HTML, CSS, JavaScript/jQuery, responsive design, popup/modal or lightbox functionality, and experience with image hotspots or interactive image maps. Please share examples of similar interactive WordPress work if available.
